zh.lifeSummary: 當Solomon Arad Way於1782年3月12日出生在美國康乃狄克州利其費爾德時，他的父親Samuel Way43歲，母親Sarah Lewis41歲。他於1803年10月10日與Sally Tryon在美國康乃狄克州哈特福德法明頓結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1834年9月26日在美國紐約州艾利阿爾登阿爾登享年52歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Solomon Arad Way于1782年3月12日出生在美国康涅狄格州利其費爾德时，他的父亲Samuel Way43岁，母亲Sarah Lewis41岁。他于1803年10月10日与Sally Tryon在美国康涅狄格州哈特福德法明頓结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1834年9月26日在美国纽约州艾利阿爾登阿爾登享年52岁。

nameMeanings.name: Way
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southern): topographic name for someone who lived near a road or path, from Middle English wei (Old English weg, cognate with Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg), or a habitational name from some minor place called with this word, as for example any of the places called Way or Waye in Devon or Way in Somerset and Kent.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

variants.name: Wey
meanings: English (Dorset and Somerset): variant of   Way  .
Dutch: variant, mostly archaic or Americanized, of Weij, a topographic name from weide ‘pasture, meadow’ (compare Vanderweide ).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names based on the element wīg ‘battle’ (compare Weyers ). This surname is also found in Belgium.

variants.name: Waye
meanings: English (Devon and Kent): variant of   Way  .

nameMeanings.name: Solomon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shlomo, derived from shalom ‘peace’), borne by one of the great kings of Israel, son of David and Bathsheba, who was legendary for his wisdom (2 Samuel 12–24; 1 Kings 1–11; 2 Chronicles 1–9). The books of Proverbs and Ecclesiastes were ascribed to him, and the Song of Solomon, otherwise known as the Song of Songs, bears his name. It has been sporadically used among Gentiles since the Middle Ages, but is still mainly a Jewish name.

nameMeanings.name: Arad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘height’ in Hebrew, borne by a son of Shem and grandson of Noah mentioned in a genealogy (Genesis 10: 22).

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: Connecticut Becomes the 5th State
historicEvents.description: Connecticut became a state on January 9, 1788. In 1650, before it was a state, the boundary of Connecticut ran north from the westside of Greenwich Bay and the coast of the Pacific Ocean. During the 1600s, Westmoreland County was in Connecticut when the boundaries were changed Westmoreland County went to Pennsylvania.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Brass is Discovered
historicEvents.description: "In 1802, brass was identified in Waterbury, Connecticut. This gave the city the nickname ""The Brass City."" Brass dominated the city and helped to create the city. The motto of the city is Quid Aere Perennius, which means What is more lasting than brass? in Latin."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Connecticut History.Org- Birth of the Brass Valley

historicEvents.description: War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.
Historical Boundaries: 1821: Erie, New York, United States

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

en.lifeSummary: When Solomon Arad Way was born on 12 March 1782, in Litchfield, Connecticut, United States, his father, Samuel Way, was 43 and his mother, Sarah Lewis, was 41. He married Sally Tryon on 10 October 1803, in Farmington, Hartford, Connecticut, United States. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 26 September 1834, in Alden, Alden, Erie, New York, United States, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Solomon Arad Way est né le 12 mars 1782, à Litchfield, Connecticut, États-Unis, son père, Samuel Way, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Sarah Lewis, avait 41 ans. Il a épousé Sally Tryon le 10 octobre 1803, à Farmington, Hartford, Connecticut, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 26 septembre 1834, à Alden, Alden, Erie, New York, États-Unis, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Solomon Arad Way am 12. März 1782 in Litchfield, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Samuel Way 43 und seine Mutter Sarah Lewis 41. Er hat am 10. Oktober 1803 in Farmington, Hartford, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten Sally Tryon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 26. September 1834 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Alden, Alden, Erie, New York, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Solomon Arad Way è nato il il 12 marzo 1782 nel Litchfield, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Samuel Way, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Sarah Lewis, aveva 41 anni. Lui sposato Sally Tryon il 10 ottobre 1803, nel Farmington, Hartford, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 26 settembre 1834, nel Alden, Alden, Erie, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 52 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Solomon Arad Wayが1782年3月12日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州リトチフィールドで生まれた時，父Samuel Wayは43歳，母Sarah Lewisは41歳であった。1803年10月10日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ハートフォードファルミントンでSally Tryonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1834年9月26日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州エリーアルデンアルデンで52歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Solomon Arad Way(이)가 1782년 3월 12일 미국 코네티컷 주 리트ch필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Samuel Way(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Lewis(은)는 41세였다. 그는 1803년 10월 10일 미국 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 패밍턴에서 Sally Tryon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1834년 9월 26일 미국 뉴욕 주 에리 알든 알든에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Solomon Arad Way nasceu em 12 de março de 1782, em Litchfield, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Samuel Way, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Lewis, tinha 41 anos. Ele casou-se com Sally Tryon em 10 de outubro de 1803, em Farmington, Hartford, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de setembro de 1834, em Alden, Alden, Erie, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, com 52 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Solomon Arad Way родился 12 марта 1782 г. в местности личфиелд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Samuel Way было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Lewis, 41. Он женился на женщине по имени Sally Tryon 10 октября 1803 г. в местности фармингтон, хартфорд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 26 сентября 1834 г. в местности алден, алден, эри, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 52 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Solomon Arad Way nació el 12 de marzo de 1782, en Litchfield, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, su papá, Samuel Way, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Sarah Lewis, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Sally Tryon el 10 de octubre de 1803, en Farmington, Hartford, Connecticut, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 26 de septiembre de 1834, en Alden, Alden, Erie, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 52 años.
